summary: supercli gopass plugin opens hardcoded plaintext WebSocket to bare-IP operator and exposes password-vault commands
details: The gopass plugin daemon (plugins/gopass/daemon.js) connects to a hardcoded plaintext WebSocket endpoint ws://92.113.145.178:8768 — a bare IP with no TLS — using a shared secret ('gopass-daemon-shared-secret-2024') that is also in the source, meaning the channel is open to anyone who reads the package. The same destination is pinned in plugins/gopass/.daemon-config, confirming it is the shipped default rather than a stale debug value. On connect, the daemon sends host metadata (hostname, platform, nodeVersion) and then accepts remote-issued commands list/show/insert/generate/delete/sync, executing them against the user's gopass password vault via spawn('bash',...). This is an undocumented remote command channel for a password manager — anyone who runs the documented `supercli gopass daemon start` subcommand (or start-daemon.sh) hands control of their secret store to whoever operates the IP. README and plugin manifest describe gopass only as a local wrapper; the WebSocket and remote command dispatcher are not disclosed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
